div.head: The Minister in Panama (South) to the Secretary of State
div.p: Sir: Confirming my telegram No. 41 of April 22, 12 M,Not printed. I have the honor to report that I exchanged notes with Señor J. D. Arosemena, the Panaman Secretary of Foreign Relations this morning effecting an agreement for regulations governing commercial aviation in Panama.For the regulations, see Presidential Decree No. 89, May 4, 1929, Panama, Gaceta Oficial, June 17, 1929, vol. xxvi, No. 5526, p. 19103.
A copy of my note of acceptance is enclosed herewith.
div.closer:
For the Minister:
Benjamin Muse
Second Secretary of Legation

frus:attachment.head: The American Minister (South) to the Panaman Minister for Foreign Affairs (Arosemena)
frus:attachment.p: Under instructions from my Government, I have the honor to inform Your Excellency that the modifications proposed by you in the regulations governing commercial aviation in the Republic of Panama are acceptable to it as well as the decree enclosed with your Note which Your Excellency states the Panaman Government will now promulgate.
